title: Stereospermum personatum
taxonomy: Stereospermum personatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae, which is a large and diverse family of plants commonly known as the Bignonia or trumpetflower family. The Bignoniaceae family includes over 800 species, many of which are native to the tropics and subtropics of the Americas, Africa, and Asia. Stereospermum personatum is a climbing vine with heart-shaped leaves and trumpet-shaped flowers, which are typically white or yellow in color. The plant is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and is often found in forests and woodlands. The classification of Stereospermum personatum places it in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Lamiales.

intro: Stereospermum personatum, also known as the Yellow Bellacantha, is a tropical flowering plant native to Southeast Asia and the Indian subcontinent. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
